pubmed_links: [Ni et al. (2021) LncRNA-AC006129.1 reactivates a SOCS3-mediated anti-inflammatory response through DNA methylation-mediated CIC downregulation in schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32015466/);[Luo et al. (2025) Association of Serum SOCS3 and Inflammatory Marker Levels With Cognitive Function in First-Episode Schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40545471/);[Tsukada et al. (2015) The suppression of maternal-fetal leukemia inhibitory factor signal relay pathway by maternal immune activation impairs brain development in mice](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26043040/)
summary: SOCS3, a suppressor of cytokine signaling that negatively regulates JAK/STAT inflammatory pathways, is downregulated in first-episode schizophrenia and negatively correlates with cognitive impairment; a schizophrenia-associated lncRNA (AC006129.1) promotes SOCS3 expression via epigenetic CIC suppression, and maternal immune activation-induced placental SOCS3 upregulation disrupts fetal LIF signaling and neurogenesis, linking SOCS3-mediated neuroinflammatory signaling to both psychiatric disease and neurodevelopmental risk. (emerging evidence)
